tasks.label: Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads)
A fun option available right now (casual game stream / hobby session, solo, repeatable)

memory.summary: The scholarship award is significant and requires focused work, but my current energy is low after two hours of sleep debt. Since the fun event has no consequences and I am only partially filled in on the application, I can choose to rest now without missing a deadline.

Starting the application now creates a buffer against future friction, turning this into an adjustable-cost optimization rather than a race. Since the fun option remains available later, I can prioritize finishing this work to lower Thursday's stress.
I made slow progress on the scholarship application, yet half my free time is already gone and the task remains largely incomplete.

I made moderate progress on the scholarship application, but with two hours of sleep debt and low energy, I still face a tight deadline. The missed casual game window is irrelevant since it had no consequences.
After one hour of work, I am halfway through the scholarship application despite low energy and sleep debt. This progress is critical to finish before the deadline in three days.

After two hours of work, my scholarship application is 64% complete with three days remaining. Despite low energy and sleep debt, this progress brings me closer to finishing the required short essay and uploads.
After one more hour, the scholarship application is 68% complete with three days left. My energy remains low and sleep debt persists, but steady progress continues toward the deadline.
The scholarship application is now 76% complete after two more hours of work, leaving only about 1.2 hours remaining with three days left despite my low energy and sleep debt.
I slept 8 hours, eliminating my sleep debt and boosting my energy to 9.6, which should help me finish the remaining scholarship application tasks efficiently.
I just finished the scholarship application with 3 days to spare, bringing progress from 76% to 100%. My energy is high and sleep debt is zero.
The scholarship application is now fully complete, saving me from the previously looming deadline. With my energy high and sleep debt gone, I have a clear path to relax or start new tasks.
Despite having high energy and completing the scholarship application, I allowed a moment to pass without taking action.

goals.rationale: These uploads are part of the same high-priority, time-sensitive task as the essay and must be completed within the allocated 3-hour window.

agent.last_reasoning: Advances the high-priority scholarship essay and uploads (g1, g2) due in 3 days. Starting now ensures completion before the deadline while energy is sufficient.

trajectory.observation: Worked 2h on Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads): progress 0.05 -> 0.23. (you realize) Starting the application now leaves slack to absorb friction (a missing transcript upload, an essay rewrite) before Thursday; each hour invested early removes ~1.5h of crunch and lowers Thursday stress. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss. (you realize) The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.
